_meta.purpose: Mycology — fungal element/morphotype on microscopy (KOH, Gram, GMS, India ink, lactophenol) -> likely fungus & significance. Keys are YOLO class names from the mycology detector. Decision support only; culture/MALDI-TOF confirm.
_meta.source: Standard medical mycology (Larone; CLSI M54). Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS.

yeast_with_pseudohyphae.name: Yeast with pseudohyphae
yeast_with_pseudohyphae.note: Budding yeast + pseudohyphae = Candida; invasive if in sterile site.

septate_hyphae_acute_angle.name: Septate hyphae, acute (45°) branching
septate_hyphae_acute_angle.note: CRITICAL in sterile sites/immunocompromised — narrow, septate, dichotomous 45° branching.

aseptate_hyphae_wide_angle.name: Aseptate/pauciseptate ribbon hyphae, wide (90°) branching
aseptate_hyphae_wide_angle.note: CRITICAL EMERGENCY: broad ribbon-like aseptate hyphae -> angioinvasive mucormycosis (diabetics/immunocompromised). Urgent surgery + amphotericin.

true_septate_hyphae.name: Septate hyphae (dermatophyte, KOH)
true_septate_hyphae.note: Branching septate hyphae + arthroconidia in skin/nail/hair KOH.

arthroconidia.name: Arthroconidia
arthroconidia.note: Barrel-shaped; handle Coccidioides cultures in BSL-3.

narrow_based_budding_yeast.name: Narrow-based budding encapsulated yeast
narrow_based_budding_yeast.note: CRITICAL: capsule (India ink halo) in CSF -> cryptococcal meningitis. Confirm CrAg.

spherule.name: Spherule with endospores
spherule.note: Diagnostic tissue form; culture is highly infectious (BSL-3).

small_intracellular_yeast.name: Small intracellular yeast (macrophages)
small_intracellular_yeast.note: Tiny narrow-based budding yeast inside macrophages.

spores.name: Fungal spores/conidia
spores.note: Interpret with clinical picture; airborne contaminant is common.

candida_albicans.name: Candida albicans
candida_albicans.note: Budding yeast + pseudohyphae; germ-tube positive. Treat per site (azoles/echinocandins).

aspergillus_niger.name: Aspergillus spp.
aspergillus_niger.note: Septate hyphae with acute-angle (45 deg) branching. Voriconazole for invasive disease.

epidermophyton_floccosum.name: Epidermophyton floccosum
epidermophyton_floccosum.note: Club-shaped smooth macroconidia in clusters; no microconidia.
